what_it_does: Defines a single radial-view BINDING dict named 'by_lens' whose sectors are the rows of the `projections` registry (the corpus lenses), plus a PROJECTION_SPACE_META dict that gives operator-facing human names and one-line 'is' descriptions for each projection space (common_knowledge, history, principles, topics, worldview, repo, operators, what, claimed_status, and the '—' remainder).
observations: BINDING has id 'by_lens', label, a meta block (name/is/fills/why), angle_from='projections', radius_from='time', order_by='count'.
order_by='count' is explicitly documented as an honest fallback because the projection rows have no inter-row edges to define a real sequence.
PROJECTION_SPACE_META maps 9 named spaces plus '—' (Unfiled) to {name, is} pairs.
'operators' is deliberately displayed as 'Roles' so a human operator does not read the sector as being about themselves (name != id).

Long header comments explain why the meta lives here in bindings/ rather than in projections/_meta.py (a sys.path collision with runtime/projections.py would make projections._meta import silently fail).
standouts: The 'registry-is-truth' claim: dropping a projections/<id>.py file makes a new sector appear with no code edit.
The naming subtlety where 'operators' renders as 'Roles' to avoid misreading by the human operator.
The documented sys.path collision rationale for co-locating PROJECTION_SPACE_META with the binding.
conventions: Follows the {id: {name, is}} legibility shape it cites in kinds/raw.py, nodes/_meta.py, bindings/grouped.py.
Marks human-meaning text as TENTATIVE draft pending Tim/DNA ratification.
Operator-law: avoids machine vocabulary like 'lens / embed / vector space' in operator-facing strings.
notes: A view-binding that turns the projections registry's own rows into sectors, with co-located operator-friendly labels and a documented import-collision reason for keeping them here.
questions: When (if ever) will inter-row edges exist for projection rows so order_by can be something other than 'count'?
Who or what is 'DNA' in the 'Tim/DNA ratify' ratification step?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is an angle-from-a-registry binding over the projections lenses and that is exactly what it declares.
apparent_themes: Registry-as-source-of-truth (data drives sectors, no code edits)
Operator-legibility / translation away from machine vocabulary
Honest representation (count fallback declared rather than faking an order)
intention_signals: Trying to render the system's own set of viewing-lenses as a first-class navigable view, where adding a lens to the registry automatically surfaces it, while keeping every label readable to a non-technical operator.
novelty: The self-referential move of treating the projection registry's rows as the sectors of a view, plus the deliberate id-vs-display divergence ('operators'->'Roles').
contribution: Contributes the lens-of-lenses binding and the operator-facing translation table for projection spaces, including the documented co-location rationale and the count-fallback honesty note.
summary_for_embedding: A Python binding definition that renders the rows of the projections registry as sectors of a radial 'ways of looking' view, with operator-friendly name/is descriptions for each corpus lens.
intention_for_embedding: To let the system display and switch between its own set of viewing lenses as a registry-driven, operator-legible map without code changes when lenses are added.
